claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 21 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. 

I understand you have one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and lives with you at home.

To clarify Alex's situation: Is Alex currently in any approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any approved education or training. They work full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 21 years old
- Alex is not in approved education or training
- Alex works full-time

Unfortunately, this means you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Let me explain why:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Decision: Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

Child Benefit is available for:
- Children under 16, OR
- Children aged 16-19 (under 20) who stay in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 years old, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit, regardless of whether they live with you or their employment status.

Child Benefit would have stopped when Alex either turned 20 or left approved education/training (whichever came first).

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they exceed the maximum age limit of 20 for children in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 (under 20) who remain in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or employment status.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, they exceed the maximum age limit of 20 for children in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and working full-time. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 (under 20) who remain in approved education or training. As Alex is 21, they are above the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of their education or employment status.
